rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing paper's empirical design, especially Study 6, where the authors explicitly ground their manipulation of binary brand gender personality (logo design/typeface) in Jones, Kidwell, and Hamby (2024). They also cite it when describing the masculinity/femininity perception measures used in the pretest, indicating methodological borrowing rather than a generic background mention. Although the citation is not central to the main theory of gender-neutral marketing, it plays a clear functional role in how a key boundary-condition study is operationalized.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited paper's brand-gender operationalization and related measurement precedent in its Study 6 boundary-condition test. In particular, it uses Jones et al. (2024) to justify manipulating and assessing masculine versus feminine brand cues.
